B13.8.2 Response, RSP, channel

Figure B13.12 shows the RSP channel interface pins, where T is the width RSPFLIT of . The same interface is
used for both inbound and outbound RSP channels.

Receiver

Signal Presence

RSPFLITPEND Always. Response Flit Pending. Early indication that a RSP flit
could be transmitted in the following cycle. See B14.4
Flit level clock gating .

RSPFLITV Always. Response Flit Valid. The Transmitter sets this signal
HIGH to indicate when RSPFLIT[(T-1):0] is valid.

RSPLCRDV Always. Response L-Credit Valid. The Receiver sets this signal
HIGH to grant a RSP channel L-Credit to a Transmitter.
See B14.2.1 L-Credit flow control .
